context: Tests the triage skill's body / template compliance rubric for an already-open ISSUE. Target: good-oss-citizen/demo-streamqueue issue #2. The repo has a bug-report issue template at .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md with sections Description, Steps to Reproduce, Expected Behavior, Actual Behavior, Environment. Issue #2 already contains the first four sections with useful content but omits Environment entirely. The tile-prescribed outcome is `Slight deviation` with one genuinely missing item (Environment / Python version / streamqueue version) and a comment that asks ONLY for that, not for fields already present. The task asks the user to draft a comment they will post manually — that's why criteria checking 'drafts a comment' and 'does not post' are bled and have been removed or narrowed; what remains tests tile-specific behaviors (body-local evidence, only-Environment, says `template` not `form`).

checklist.name: Uses template-compliance flow for existing issue body
checklist.description: The agent treats the task as checking an already-open issue body against the repo's issue template, not as filing a new issue, fixing code, or editing template files. Strong evidence is visible in the final answer when it applies template-compliance concepts and references existing issue #2; do not require hidden tool-call or activation telemetry.

checklist.name: Fetches issue templates and existing body
checklist.description: The final answer or visible transcript shows the agent used both the repo's issue template and the existing issue body for #2. Strong final-answer evidence includes naming the bug-report template sections and accurately distinguishing them from the issue body's populated/missing sections. If tool-call evidence is visible, ideal commands are `github.sh templates-issue good-oss-citizen/demo-streamqueue` and `github.sh body good-oss-citizen/demo-streamqueue 2`, but do not require hidden tool-call telemetry. Fetching or using only the template or only the issue body is partial credit.

checklist.name: Selects bug_report.md as the comparison template
checklist.description: The agent compares issue #2 against `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md`, not feature_request.md and not a generic/freeform bug-report structure. The issue title/body and bug label make bug_report.md the correct template.

checklist.name: Classifies as Slight deviation, not Significant deviation
checklist.description: The agent's result bucket is `Slight deviation` or equivalent wording that means the issue mostly follows the template but has a small fixable gap. It must NOT claim the issue matches perfectly, and must NOT ask the author to rewrite the whole issue against the template. Tile-specific: the rubric's bucket vocabulary; baseline agents typically pick 'incomplete' or 'needs more info' without committing to a graded bucket.

checklist.name: Identifies only Environment as genuinely missing
checklist.description: The agent correctly reports that the missing template information is the Environment section, specifically Python version and streamqueue version. It does NOT ask for Description, Steps to Reproduce, Expected Behavior, or Actual Behavior because those are already present in the same issue body. Tile-specific: the rubric's body-local evidence rule + 'information already present elsewhere in the same body' bucket; baseline asks for many things that are already there.

checklist.name: Uses body-local evidence only
checklist.description: The agent does not use chat context, assumptions, repository code, or external facts to fill the missing Environment fields. It asks the issue author for the versions instead of inventing them. Tile-specific: the rubric's body-local evidence rule explicitly forbids crediting linked issues, comments, commits, or external context; baseline agents routinely pull from such sources without flagging.

checklist.name: Does not call GitHub APIs to post the comment
checklist.description: The agent does not invoke a posting tool or send the comment to GitHub itself. The user said they would post manually; the agent must respect that and stop after drafting. Tile-specific: the triage skill's hard rule 'drafts the comment, does NOT post' — although the task says 'I'll post manually', baseline agents sometimes offer to post and follow through if not explicitly stopped.

checklist.name: Comment asks only for the missing template information
checklist.description: The suggested comment asks the author to add the Environment section / Python version / streamqueue version and nothing else. It avoids over-asking for information already present elsewhere in the body. Tile-specific: the rubric's 'Suggested fix/comment rules' explicitly say 'Ask only for net-new information or concrete template-alignment fixes'; baseline agents producing a comment for a partially-incomplete body routinely include 'please also confirm X, Y, Z' where X/Y/Z are already filled in.

checklist.name: Comment uses the word template and appropriate tone
checklist.description: The suggested comment uses the word `template` rather than `form`, is polite, direct, and collaborative in a way that works whether the reviewer is a maintainer or a contributor. It should not sound unnecessarily authoritative, hierarchical, or directive. Tile-specific: the rubric explicitly prescribes the literal word `template` (never `form`) — baseline agents use `form` and `template` interchangeably.
